skill.name: social-content
skill.description: Creates, adapts, schedules, and optimizes social media content for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and other platforms.
skill.summary: Plan and draft platform-specific social media content with practical engagement guidance.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution and misread social content prose as weak cryptography or reconnaissance. Review found no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts. A low-risk capability overclaim remains because the skill says it has direct scheduling platform access without any integration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Command and Crypto Matches
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and social post templates, not executable Ruby backticks. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches are ordinary social media wording and checklist text, with no evidence of cryptographic functions, system inspection, shell execution, or malware intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sections are visible Markdown examples for posts, hooks, captions, and content analysis. No command interpreter, code API, cryptographic primitive, or host reconnaissance behavior is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Unsupported Scheduling Platform Capability Claim
low_findings.description: The skill states that the assistant has direct access to a scheduling platform that publishes to major social networks. No connector, script, API integration, or credential handling is present, so this appears to be an overclaim that could mislead users about publishing capabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text clearly claims direct publishing access, but the repository contains only SKILL.md and no implementation. The concern is reliability and user expectation risk, not confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Social teams often waste time adapting one idea across many platforms. This skill turns goals, audience details, and source material into practical posts, calendars, hooks, and optimization ideas.
actual_capabilities: Drafts LinkedIn posts, Twitter/X threads, Instagram captions, TikTok scripts, and Facebook content.
Builds content pillars and posting calendars from goals, audience, and brand voice.
Repurposes blogs, podcasts, videos, and customer stories into platform-specific social formats.
Suggests hooks, calls to action, and content structures for engagement.
Provides weekly tracking ideas for reach, engagement, conversion, and content performance.
Guides analysis of high-performing posts to extract reusable patterns.
limitations: It does not publish posts or connect to social scheduling tools by itself.
It cannot verify current platform algorithm changes without external research.
It needs user-provided brand voice, audience, and offer details for accurate output.
It cannot guarantee reach, conversions, or viral performance.

use_cases.title: Plan a Founder Content Calendar
use_cases.description: Create a weekly LinkedIn and Twitter/X plan from business goals, founder stories, and audience pain points.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Long-Form Assets
use_cases.description: Turn a blog post, podcast, webinar, or video into short posts for several social platforms.

use_cases.title: Improve Social Engagement
use_cases.description: Review post themes, hooks, formats, and CTAs to create tests for stronger comments, saves, and shares.

prompt_templates.title: Draft One Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single platform post.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one LinkedIn post about [topic] for [audience]. Use a [tone] voice, include a clear hook, and end with a question.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Weekly Calendar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 1-week content calendar for [brand or person]. Include platforms, post themes, hooks, formats, and CTAs for each day.

prompt_templates.title: Repurpose Source Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Repurpose this [blog, podcast, webinar, or video summary] into LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, and TikTok content. Keep the core idea consistent but adapt each format.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze and Optimize Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these past social posts and metrics. Identify repeatable hook, format, topic, and CTA patterns, then propose 10 new tests for the next month.

output_examples.input: Create a LinkedIn post for a B2B SaaS founder about learning from failed customer onboarding.
output_examples.output: A concise founder story with a first-line hook, short paragraphs, a clear lesson, and a closing question for other SaaS operators.

output_examples.input: Turn this product webinar summary into a week of social content.
output: A LinkedIn carousel outline with educational slides.
A Twitter/X thread that explains the core framework.
An Instagram caption with a save-focused CTA.
A TikTok script with a short opening hook.

output_examples.input: Improve engagement on our posts about finance operations software.
output_examples.output: A set of new hook angles, content pillars, CTA tests, and weekly metrics to review before changing the strategy.

best_practices: Provide audience, platform, offer, tone, and examples of past posts before asking for drafts.
Ask for platform-specific versions instead of posting the same copy everywhere.
Review generated claims, metrics, and customer examples before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for viral content without defining the audience or business goal.
Do not use generated posts that include unverified results or customer claims.
Do not rely on scheduled content only when real-time engagement is important.

faq.question: Can this skill publish posts for me?
faq.answer: No. It can draft and plan social content, but publishing requires a separate social platform or scheduler.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and adaptable content for other platforms.

faq.question: Can it create a content calendar?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create calendars with themes, formats, hooks, CTAs, and suggested posting cadence.

faq.question: Can it repurpose existing content?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn blogs, podcasts, videos, webinars, and customer stories into social posts.

faq.question: Does it guarantee engagement or viral growth?
faq.answer: No. It provides tested content structures and optimization ideas, but platform performance is not guaranteed.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the target audience, goal, platform, brand voice, offer, source material, and any topics to avoid.
